title: Chapter Fifty-Eight: Great Hero of Desolate Sect
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"Dan Jeong-jeong!"

They heard the girl's name as they left Pingyin County. She had shown strong determination even after witnessing the carnage -- composed, unshaken, offering dried tofu skin to Yeon-shin with a warmth that was almost painful to receive.

He took one bite. Then he left.

*She will live well. She has the qualities for it.*

"Our Lightning Flash would be the best candidate for a husband," Cheong Myeong said, repeating the old joke from the Righteous Sword Family mission.

No one laughed.

Back then, Hyeon Won-chang would have bantered back. Would have said something ridiculous, something warm, something that made the road feel shorter. Yeon-shin could hear the absent voice so clearly it hurt -- a door inside him rattling on its hinges, threatening to swing wide.

Not now.

They ran. Three Radiant Demon Wing blue-ranked masters, wind streams scattering behind them. They needed relay station horses for the long distance. Unseasonable sandstorms spread on the main road, earth-colored dust spreading to their sides and back, speaking of urgency.

"Are those martial families strong enough to handle five white ranks?" Yeon-shin asked, his voice carrying true qi against the fierce wind. "Regular sects should not have been a problem."

"They could not exert full power," Cheong Myeong answered, his voice rumbling with inner power. "Obvious, since some were taken alive."

*Because of my sound attack's aftermath...*

"It is because the white-ranked juniors' martial arts were shallow. When you are not stronger than the enemy, you have no choice but to take care of yourself. That is the murim."

Cold words. True words.

"Either way, it is something blue ranks must handle," Mi-ryeo said, her face its usual mask. "We must split at the relay station. The main force has no leeway. Exterminating one of the Eight Great Families is no ordinary matter."

"They went down to South Zhili? The blood demons."

"There are many paths to keep in mind. About four routes," Cheong Myeong said.

Silence fell between them, filled only by wind and footfall. Yeon-shin was thinking.

"But is Desolate Sect not the strongest in the martial world?" he asked. "With what confidence did the family sects make surprise attacks?"

"One of two things. Our methods are quite cold, yes? Parents whose children are taken -- they go mad. Some family heads give up their children entirely. The other possibility?" Cheong Myeong's handsome features showed no expression. "They know. That our Desolate Sect has no leeway."

"What do you mean?"

"Watching the entire Central Plains. Governing a martial world the size of fifteen or sixteen small countries? It is an impossible duty. We are always short-handed. Any sect with reasonably quick intelligence knows this. They rarely show fear."

The relay station appeared. They jumped the roof. Yeon-shin showed the Desolate token to the startled administrator.

"It is genuine! Take them!"

Mounting the borrowed horse, Yeon-shin spoke carefully.

"I do not understand why they took them without killing. Would Won-chang still be alive?"

"Usually it would end in death," Cheong Myeong said, already on horseback. "After taking down a Desolate warrior, who would keep them alive? That is why capturing them is different. There must be intent."

"What intent?"

Mi-ryeo's black pupils glanced at him. Cheong Myeong's blue eyes did the same.

"I think it is those who were pursuing since the Dragon Phoenix Gathering," Mi-ryeo said. "The movements connect."

"The captured white ranks might just be means," Cheong Myeong added. "Is the Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm not the issue? They want to kill you by any means."

It made sense. These were the ones who had mobilized thunderclap bombs. His seniors' speculation struck sharply.

"Finding the white ranks comes first," Cheong Myeong said, tone light as he stretched his legs on horseback. "But avoid getting caught in power struggles. The Namgung family is at war with the Blood Flame Sect. Those evil sect bastards spread their Heaven-Spanning Net in their inner courtyard of Huizhou."

"Since they were captured by blood demons, they will live for ten days," Mi-ryeo said. "They are ones who steal true qi through blood techniques. Main sect warriors are rare prey."

Her obsidian pupils held concern as she looked at him.

Time to split up.

Yeon-shin gave a brief cupped fist salute on horseback and whipped his horse without looking back. The mission continued.

*They said rescuing companions would be recorded as merit.*

It did not feel meaningful. At this moment, his heart did not speak of merit.

*Won-chang.*

The door rattled. He did not shut it. Not this time. He let it crack open, just enough -- and the feeling that came through was not grief. Not yet.

It was purpose. Sharp as a blade. Cold as Northern Light in its scabbard.

*Be safe until I get there. Until I can call you a Desolate Divine Hero.*

He rode south, alone, and the dust of the road swallowed him.

---
